body:
### Proposal to improve performance

_No response_

### Report of performance regression

## Summary

Benchmarked `vllm/vllm-omni:v0.24.0` against `v0.22.0` on Qwen3-TTS (0.6B/1.7B Base + CustomVoice) and Voxtral-4B, H200, TP=1. v0.24.0 is faster single-stream (TTFP ~unchanged at c=1), but **TTFP (time-to-first-packet) regresses 27–41% at concurrency ≥8**.

**TTFP is the focus of this report** because it is duration-independent. v0.24.0 also generates ~8.0s mean audio per request vs ~6.2s on v0.22.0 for the same prompts, so the higher end-to-end latency (E2EL) and lower audio-seconds-per-second throughput are largely explained by longer outputs and are **expected, not regressions** — they are included below only as context. TTFP does not depend on output length, and it still degrades sharply under load.

## Benchmark setup

- **Images:** `vllm/vllm-omni:v0.22.0`, `vllm/vllm-omni:v0.24.0`
- **Hardware:** NVIDIA H200, TP=1, `--gpu-memory-utilization 0.45` (two-stage LLM + vocoder pipeline sharing GPU memory)
- **Models:** `Qwen/Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-{1.7B,0.6B}-{Base,CustomVoice}`, `mistralai/Voxtral-4B-TTS-2603`
- **Bench:** `vllm-omni bench serve --omni --backend openai-audio-speech --endpoint /v1/audio/speech --request-rate inf --num-prompts 100 --max-concurrency {1,4,8,16,25}` — seed-tts / seed-tts-text datasets
- **Coverage:** 50 runs total, 0 failures

## TTFP regression (Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-Base, mean)

| c | TTFP (ms) 0.22 → 0.24 | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | 67 → 66 | ~flat |
| 4 | 90 → 99 | +10% |
| 8 | 207 → 223 | +7.7% |
| 16 | 957 → 1297 | +35.5% |
| 25 | 2100 → 2890 | +37.6% |

0.6B-Base is worse: **TTFP +41.2% at c=25**.

## Context (duration-dependent, expected — not the regression being reported)

v0.24.0 emits ~8.0s mean audio/request vs ~6.2s on v0.22.0 for identical prompts (presumably a codec/sampling change). This accounts for the higher E2EL and the audio-seconds-per-second throughput crossover at high concurrency, so those metrics are not treated as regressions here. TTFP, being duration-independent, is the clean signal.

## Impact

For real-time voice serving, TTFP is the UX-critical metric. On v0.22 a single replica held TTFP ≤~210ms up to c=8; on v0.24 the same knee produces >220ms at c=8 and >1.2s at c=16 — a materially worse time-to-first-audio under contention.

## Hypotheses (unverified — happy to help bisect)

- **Hypothesis:** Scheduler/batching change in the token-generation stage penalizing time-to-first-audio-chunk under content

comments.author: amy-why-3459
comments.body: Can you try using the latest commit-id?

title: [Performance Regression] v0.24.0: TTS time-to-first-packet (TTFP) degrades 27–41% at concurrency ≥8 vs v0.22.0 (Qwen3-TTS, H200)
